_meta.description: Standardized detection rules DB mapped by ATT&CK technique and tool

Suricata.name: Suspicious Office Document Download
Suricata.query: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HOME_NET any (msg:"Suspicious Office File Download"; fileext:"docm"; sid:1000001;)
Suricata.description: Detects HTTP download of macro-enabled Office documents (.docm) from external networks.
Suricata.false_positive_note: Legitimate business file downloads; tune by whitelisting known internal servers.
Suricata.author: Emerging Threats

Sysmon.name: Office Application Spawning Shell
Sysmon.query: EventID=1 AND ParentImage='*\OUTLOOK.EXE' AND (Image='*\cmd.exe' OR Image='*\powershell.exe')
Sysmon.description: Detects Office app spawning cmd/powershell, indicating macro execution from phishing attachment.
Sysmon.tuning_hint: Exclude legitimate Outlook automation from trusted add-ins.
Sysmon.false_positive_note: Outlook integration with IT scripts; verify parent-child process chain.
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Wazuh.name: Office Macro Execution Alert
Wazuh.description: Wazuh rule detecting Outlook spawning shell processes via Sysmon EventID 1 forwarded logs.
Wazuh.tuning_hint: Increase level to 14 for high-severity environments; add process exclusions via <field negate>.
Wazuh.author: Wazuh

Sigma.name: Spearphishing Attachment Execution
Sigma.query: EventID=1 AND ParentImage='*\OUTLOOK.EXE' AND Image='*\cmd.exe' OR Image='*\powershell.exe'
Sigma.description: Sigma rule for detecting macro-based phishing attachment execution via Office shell spawn.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

Sysmon.name: Browser Spawned from Email Client
Sysmon.query: EventID=1 AND ParentImage='*\OUTLOOK.EXE' AND (Image='*\chrome.exe' OR Image='*\msedge.exe')
Sysmon.description: Detects browser launched by Outlook, suggesting user clicked a spearphishing link in email.
Sysmon.false_positive_note: Common in legitimate email workflows; corroborate with URL reputation.
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Sigma.name: Spearphishing Link Clicked
Sigma.query: EventID=1 AND ParentImage='*\OUTLOOK.EXE' AND Image='*\chrome.exe' OR Image='*\msedge.exe'
Sigma.description: Low-confidence Sigma rule for browser launch from email client — correlate with proxy/URL logs.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

Suricata.name: Log4j JNDI Lookup Execution
Suricata.query: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HOME_NET any (msg:"Log4j JNDI Attempt"; content:"${jndi:"; sid:1000002;)
Suricata.description: Detects CVE-2021-44228 (Log4Shell) JNDI injection pattern in HTTP requests.
Suricata.false_positive_note: None expected — the ${jndi: pattern is not used in legitimate traffic.
Suricata.author: Emerging Threats

Wazuh.name: Web Server Exploitation Log4j
Wazuh.description: Wazuh rule detecting Log4j JNDI LDAP pattern in web server access logs.
Wazuh.tuning_hint: Expand regex to cover jndi:rmi and jndi:dns variants.
Wazuh.author: Wazuh

Sysmon.name: PowerShell Network Connection
Sysmon.query: EventID=3 AND Image='*\powershell.exe'
Sysmon.description: Detects PowerShell making outbound network connections — may indicate C2, download cradles, or exfiltration.
Sysmon.tuning_hint: Whitelist known update servers and internal management IPs to reduce noise.
Sysmon.false_positive_note: PowerShell-based admin tools (e.g., SCCM, Ansible) frequently generate network connections.
Sysmon.author: Microsoft Sysmon

Sysmon.name: Suspicious PowerShell CommandLine
Sysmon.query: EventID=1 AND Image='*\powershell.exe' AND (CommandLine contains '-enc' OR CommandLine contains '-nop')
Sysmon.description: Detects PowerShell with encoded (-enc) or no-profile (-nop) flags, commonly used in obfuscated malicious scripts.
Sysmon.tuning_hint: Add -w hidden and bypass to the filter for broader coverage.
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Wazuh.name: PowerShell Script Block Logging
Wazuh.description: Detects malicious PowerShell script block content (EventID 4104) forwarded via Wazuh agent.
Wazuh.tuning_hint: Extend field regex with additional malicious cmdlets: Invoke-ReflectivePEInjection, IEX.
Wazuh.author: Wazuh

Windows Event Log.name: PowerShell Script Block Logging (4104)
Windows Event Log.query: EventID=4104 AND ScriptBlockText contains suspicious patterns
Windows Event Log.description: Native PowerShell script block logging (requires GPO: Turn on PowerShell Script Block Logging).
Windows Event Log.tuning_hint: Enable in GPO: Computer Config > Admin Templates > Windows Components > PowerShell.
Windows Event Log.author: Microsoft

Sigma.name: Suspicious PowerShell Execution
Sigma.query: EventID=1 AND (CommandLine contains '-enc' OR CommandLine contains 'DownloadString')
Sigma.description: High-coverage Sigma rule for detecting encoded PowerShell and download cradles.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

T1059.003.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Windows Command Shell

Sysmon.name: Suspicious cmd.exe Child Process
Sysmon.query: EventID=1 AND Image='*\cmd.exe' AND ParentImage NOT IN known_parents
Sysmon.description: Detects cmd.exe launched from unusual parent processes such as web servers or Office apps.
Sysmon.tuning_hint: Maintain a known-good parent process list; exclude explorer.exe, services.exe, svchost.exe.
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Wazuh.name: cmd.exe Execution from Unusual Parent
Wazuh.description: Wazuh rule flagging cmd.exe spawned by anything other than explorer.exe.
Wazuh.tuning_hint: Expand negate list with legitimate parents: services.exe, svchost.exe, taskmgr.exe.
Wazuh.author: Wazuh

Sigma.name: Suspicious Command Line Execution
Sigma.query: EventID=1 AND Image='*\cmd.exe' AND (CommandLine contains '/c' OR CommandLine contains '/k')
Sigma.description: Low-confidence rule; /c and /k flags are very common. Correlate with parent process and command content.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

Sysmon.name: Scheduled Task Created via schtasks
Sysmon.query: EventID=1 AND Image='*\schtasks.exe' AND CommandLine contains '/create'
Sysmon.description: Detects creation of scheduled tasks via schtasks.exe /create. Common persistence and lateral movement vector.
Sysmon.tuning_hint: Correlate with /TR path pointing to temp/appdata directories for higher precision.
Sysmon.false_positive_note: Software installers and sysadmin scripts frequently create scheduled tasks.
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Wazuh.name: Scheduled Task Registry Modification
Wazuh.description: Monitors the TaskCache registry hive for scheduled task creation or modification.
Wazuh.tuning_hint: Raise level to 12 in high-security environments. Baseline known tasks first.
Wazuh.author: Wazuh

Windows Event Log.name: Scheduled Task Created (4698)
Windows Event Log.description: Native Windows audit event for scheduled task creation. Requires Task Scheduler audit policy enabled.
Windows Event Log.tuning_hint: Enable via: auditpol /set /subcategory:"Other Object Access Events" /success:enable
Windows Event Log.author: Microsoft

Sigma.name: Scheduled Task Creation
Sigma.query: EventID=1 AND Image='*\schtasks.exe' AND CommandLine contains '/create'
Sigma.description: Sigma rule for detecting scheduled task creation via schtasks command.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

Suricata.name: Web Shell Upload Detection
Suricata.query: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HOME_NET any (msg:"Possible Web Shell Upload"; content:"POST"; http_method; fileext:"aspx|jsp|php"; sid:1000010;)
Suricata.description: Detects HTTP POST uploads of web shell file types (.aspx, .jsp, .php) to internal web servers.
Suricata.false_positive_note: Legitimate web app deployments; tune by restricting to unexpected upload paths.
Suricata.author: Emerging Threats

Sysmon.name: Web Shell File Created by IIS
Sysmon.query: EventID=11 AND Image='*\w3wp.exe' AND TargetFilename='*.aspx'
Sysmon.description: Detects .aspx file creation by the IIS worker process (w3wp.exe). Very high-fidelity webshell indicator.
Sysmon.false_positive_note: Deployment pipelines writing to IIS wwwroot; baseline known deploy paths.
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Sigma.name: Webshell Creation
Sigma.description: High-confidence Sigma rule for webshell file creation by IIS worker process.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

Sysmon.name: Spoolsv Suspicious Child Process
Sysmon.query: EventID=1 AND ParentImage='*\spoolsv.exe' AND Image='*\cmd.exe'
Sysmon.description: Detects cmd.exe spawned by spoolsv.exe — indicator of Print Spooler exploitation (CVE-2021-1675, PrintNightmare).
Sysmon.false_positive_note: Rare in benign environments; very high confidence for exploitation attempts.
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Sigma.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
Sigma.description: Sigma rule for Print Spooler privilege escalation via child process spawning.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

Sysmon.name: CreateRemoteThread in svchost
Sysmon.query: EventID=8 AND TargetImage='*\svchost.exe'
Sysmon.description: Detects CreateRemoteThread targeting svchost.exe — a common DLL injection indicator for code execution under a trusted process.
Sysmon.tuning_hint: Correlate with Sysmon Event 7 (ImageLoad) for loaded DLL path verification.
Sysmon.false_positive_note: Security products occasionally inject into svchost; maintain a known-good DLL list.
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Sigma.name: Process Injection - DLL Injection
Sigma.description: Sigma rule detecting DLL injection via CreateRemoteThread into svchost.exe.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

T1070.001.name: Indicator Removal: Clear Windows Event Logs

Wazuh.name: Windows Audit Log Cleared
Wazuh.description: Wazuh rule detecting Windows Security log clearing (EventID 1102) — strong indicator of attacker covering tracks.
Wazuh.false_positive_note: Almost no false positives; log clearing is not a normal admin operation.
Wazuh.author: Wazuh

Windows Event Log.name: Security Log Cleared (1102)
Windows Event Log.description: Security Audit Log was cleared. Generated by wevtutil or any process calling ClearEventLog.
Windows Event Log.author: Microsoft

Windows Event Log.name: System Log Cleared (104)
Windows Event Log.description: System Event Log was cleared. Paired with 1102 for complete log clearing detection.
Windows Event Log.author: Microsoft

Sigma.name: Windows Event Log Cleared
Sigma.description: Detects clearing of Security or System event log. Near-zero false positive rate.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

Sysmon.name: LOLBin Execution Detected
Sysmon.query: EventID=1 AND (Image='*\rundll32.exe' OR Image='*\regsvr32.exe' OR Image='*\mshta.exe')
Sysmon.description: Detects execution of signed LOLBins (rundll32, regsvr32, mshta) used to proxy malicious code and evade whitelisting.
Sysmon.tuning_hint: Filter by CommandLine content — empty or URL-based arguments are most suspicious.
Sysmon.false_positive_note: rundll32 and regsvr32 are legitimately used by many applications; context is key.
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Sigma.name: System Binary Proxy Execution
Sigma.description: Sigma LOLBin rule; pair with CommandLine analysis for higher fidelity.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

Sysmon.name: LSASS Process Access
Sysmon.query: EventID=10 AND TargetImage='*\lsass.exe' AND GrantedAccess='0x1010'
Sysmon.description: Detects suspicious process access to LSASS with 0x1010 access mask, used by Mimikatz and similar tools.
Sysmon.tuning_hint: Also alert on 0x1038, 0x143A access flags. Add SourceImage filter to reduce noise from EDR processes.
Sysmon.false_positive_note: Some EDR and AV products legitimately access LSASS; maintain an allow-list by SourceImage.
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Wazuh.name: LSASS Access Detected
Wazuh.description: Wazuh rule for detecting LSASS process access with credential-dumping access flags.
Wazuh.tuning_hint: Add negate on SourceImage for known security tools to reduce alert volume.
Wazuh.author: Wazuh

Windows Event Log.name: Sensitive Privilege Use (4673)
Windows Event Log.description: Sensitive privilege use event; indicates privileged operation on LSASS which may include credential access.
Windows Event Log.author: Microsoft

Sigma.name: LSASS Memory Dump
Sigma.description: High-fidelity Sigma rule for LSASS credential dumping via process access flag inspection.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

T1550.002.name: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Pass the Hash

Windows Event Log.name: Pass the Hash Logon (4624 Type 9)
Windows Event Log.description: Detects NewCredentials logon type (9) used in Pass-the-Hash and Pass-the-Ticket attacks with NTLM/Negotiate auth.
Windows Event Log.tuning_hint: Correlate TargetUserName with known service accounts to reduce false positives.
Windows Event Log.author: Microsoft

Sigma.name: Pass the Hash Activity
Sigma.description: Sigma rule for PtH detection via NewCredentials logon type.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

Suricata.name: DNS Tunneling - Suspicious Long Query
Suricata.query: alert dns $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET 53 (msg:"Suspicious Long DNS Query"; dsize:>100; sid:1000003;)
Suricata.description: Detects unusually long DNS queries (>100 bytes) indicating DNS tunneling for C2 or data exfiltration.
Suricata.tuning_hint: Tune dsize threshold based on baseline DNS traffic; lower to >60 for stricter detection.
Suricata.author: Emerging Threats

Sysmon.name: DNS Query to Suspicious Domain
Sysmon.query: EventID=22 AND QueryName matches long/random patterns
Sysmon.description: Detects DNS queries to domains with high entropy or unusual length (DGA or DNS tunnel indicators).
Sysmon.tuning_hint: Apply entropy scoring or regex for DGA-like patterns on QueryName.
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Suricata.name: SMB Lateral Movement
Suricata.query: alert tcp $HOME_NET any -> $HOME_NET 445 (msg:"Internal SMB Traffic"; flow:to_server; sid:1000020;)
Suricata.description: Low-confidence detection of internal SMB traffic on port 445, which may indicate lateral movement.
Suricata.tuning_hint: Narrow to unexpected source/destination pairs; exclude known file servers.
Suricata.false_positive_note: Extremely noisy in Windows environments. Correlate with auth events.
Suricata.author: Emerging Threats

Windows Event Log.name: Network Logon to Admin Share (4624 Type 3)
Windows Event Log.description: Detects network logon (Type 3) used for SMB admin share access — a common lateral movement indicator.
Windows Event Log.tuning_hint: Filter by TargetUserName excluding service accounts and known management tools.
Windows Event Log.author: Microsoft

Suricata.name: Large Outbound Data Transfer
Suricata.query: alert tcp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (msg:"Large Outbound Transfer"; dsize:>5000; flow:to_server; sid:1000030;)
Suricata.description: Detects abnormally large outbound TCP transfers that may indicate data exfiltration.
Suricata.tuning_hint: Pair with threat intelligence feeds; set baseline outbound transfer thresholds per host.
Suricata.false_positive_note: Very noisy; backup and update traffic frequently exceeds 5KB. Requires behavioral baselining.
Suricata.author: Emerging Threats

Windows Event Log.name: Successful Logon After Multiple Failures
Windows Event Log.description: Detects successful logon following multiple failures, indicating successful brute force or credential stuffing.
Windows Event Log.tuning_hint: Implement correlation rule: N failures within T seconds before success on same TargetUserName.
Windows Event Log.author: Microsoft

T1547.001.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys

Sysmon.name: Registry Run Key Modification
Sysmon.query: EventID=13 AND TargetObject contains '\CurrentVersion\Run'
Sysmon.description: Detects modification of registry Run keys used for persistence. Any new entry here runs at every user logon.
Sysmon.tuning_hint: Baseline known software Run entries; alert on new TargetObject values not in whitelist.
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Wazuh.name: Run Key Registry Modified
Wazuh.description: Wazuh rule detecting writes to registry Run keys, a primary persistence mechanism.
Wazuh.tuning_hint: Add RunOnce and RunServices to the regex for broader coverage.
Wazuh.author: Wazuh

Sigma.name: Registry Run Key Persistence
Sigma.description: Sigma rule for detecting Run key persistence — covers HKLM and HKCU locations.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

Sysmon.name: System Info Commands Execution
Sysmon.query: EventID=1 AND (Image='*\systeminfo.exe' OR Image='*\hostname.exe')
Sysmon.description: Detects execution of systeminfo.exe or hostname.exe, often used during post-exploitation discovery phase.
Sysmon.tuning_hint: Correlate with other discovery commands (whoami, net user, ipconfig) within short time window.
Sysmon.false_positive_note: Common in IT management and monitoring scripts.
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Sysmon.name: Suspicious Archive Creation
Sysmon.query: EventID=1 AND (Image='*\rar.exe' OR Image='*\7z.exe') AND CommandLine contains 'a'
Sysmon.description: Detects use of rar.exe or 7z.exe to archive files, common for staging data prior to exfiltration.
Sysmon.tuning_hint: Filter by archive destination path — unusual paths (Temp, AppData) are more suspicious.
Sysmon.false_positive_note: Developers and backup tools commonly use 7z/rar.
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Sysmon.name: Mass File Rename/Encrypt Activity
Sysmon.query: EventID=11 AND TargetFilename matches encrypted extensions
Sysmon.description: Detects mass file creation with ransomware-typical extensions (.encrypted, .locked). High volume is key indicator.
Sysmon.tuning_hint: Set volume threshold; single file renames may be FP. Alert on >50 events in 60s.
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Wazuh.name: Ransomware File Activity
Wazuh.description: Wazuh FIM rule detecting file writes with ransomware extension patterns.
Wazuh.author: Wazuh

Sigma.name: Ransomware File Extension
Sigma.description: Sigma rule detecting ransomware file extension writes.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

Suricata.name: SSH Brute Force Attempt
Suricata.query: alert tcp $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HOME_NET 22 (msg:"SSH Brute Force"; threshold:type both,track by_src,count 5,seconds 60; sid:2001219;)
Suricata.description: Detects SSH brute force via threshold: 5 connections from same source in 60s.
Suricata.tuning_hint: Lower count to 3 for stricter environments; whitelist known pentest IPs.
Suricata.author: Emerging Threats

Wazuh.name: Multiple Authentication Failures
Wazuh.query:
<rule id="5710" level="10" frequency="6" timeframe="120">
  <if_matched_sid>5503</if_matched_sid>
  <description>Multiple authentication failures</description>
</rule>
Wazuh.description: Built-in Wazuh rule detecting 6+ auth failures within 120 seconds from same source.
Wazuh.author: Wazuh

Windows Event Log.name: Account Lockout (4740)
Windows Event Log.description: Account lockout event; triggered after brute force threshold exceeded.
Windows Event Log.author: Microsoft

Windows Event Log.name: Multiple Failed Logons (4625)
Windows Event Log.description: Failed logon audit event; use count correlation for brute force detection.
Windows Event Log.author: Microsoft

Sigma.name: Brute Force - Multiple Failed Logons
Sigma.description: Aggregation-based Sigma rule for brute force: count 4625 events per user.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

Sysmon.name: Browser Credential File Access
Sysmon.query: EventID=1 AND CommandLine contains 'Login Data' OR 'logins.json'
Sysmon.description: Detects access to browser credential stores (Chrome Login Data, Firefox logins.json).
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Sigma.name: Browser Credential Store Access
Sigma.query: EventID=1 AND (CommandLine contains 'Login Data' OR CommandLine contains 'cookies.sqlite')
Sigma.description: Sigma rule for detecting credential theft from browser stores.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

Wazuh.name: User Account Created
Wazuh.description: Detects new local user account creation via EventID 4720.
Wazuh.author: Wazuh

Windows Event Log.name: User Account Created (4720)
Windows Event Log.description: A new user account was created. Any unexpected account creation is suspicious.
Windows Event Log.author: Microsoft

Windows Event Log.name: User Added to Admin Group (4732)
Windows Event Log.description: A member was added to the local Administrators group (SID S-1-5-32-544).
Windows Event Log.author: Microsoft

Sigma.name: Local User Creation
Sigma.query: EventID=4720 OR (EventID=1 AND Image='*\net.exe' AND CommandLine contains 'user /add')
Sigma.description: Detects new local user via native audit or net.exe command.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

Suricata.name: Port Scan Detection
Suricata.query: alert tcp $HOME_NET any -> $HOME_NET any (msg:"Internal Port Scan"; flags:S; threshold:type both,track by_src,count 30,seconds 10; sid:2001569;)
Suricata.description: Detects internal port scanning via SYN flood threshold: 30 SYN packets in 10 seconds from same source.
Suricata.tuning_hint: Adjust count/seconds based on normal network behavior. Exclude known vulnerability scanners.
Suricata.author: Emerging Threats

Sysmon.name: Nmap Execution
Sysmon.query: EventID=1 AND (Image='*\nmap.exe' OR CommandLine contains 'nmap')
Sysmon.description: Detects nmap.exe execution or nmap in CommandLine — direct indicator of network scanning.
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Sigma.name: Network Scanning Tool Execution
Sigma.description: Detects scanning tool execution (nmap, masscan) indicating reconnaissance activity.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

Suricata.name: Suspicious File Download
Suricata.query: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HOME_NET any (msg:"Suspicious EXE Download"; fileext:"exe"; flow:to_client; sid:2001570;)
Suricata.description: Detects HTTP delivery of .exe files from external networks to internal hosts.
Suricata.tuning_hint: Combine with threat intel feed for URL/IP reputation scoring.
Suricata.author: Emerging Threats

Sysmon.name: Certutil/BITSAdmin Download
Sysmon.query: EventID=1 AND (Image='*\certutil.exe' AND CommandLine contains '-urlcache') OR (Image='*\bitsadmin.exe' AND CommandLine contains '/transfer')
Sysmon.description: Detects LOLBin file download via certutil -urlcache or bitsadmin /transfer.
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Sigma.name: Ingress Tool Transfer via LOLBin
Sigma.query: EventID=1 AND (CommandLine contains 'certutil -urlcache' OR CommandLine contains 'bitsadmin /transfer')
Sigma.description: Sigma rule for LOLBin-based file download for tool delivery.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

Sysmon.name: Process Running from Suspicious Path
Sysmon.query: EventID=1 AND (Image contains '\Temp\' OR Image contains '\AppData\') AND (OriginalFileName='cmd.exe' OR OriginalFileName='powershell.exe')
Sysmon.description: Detects system binaries (cmd, powershell) running from temp directories — common masquerading technique.
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Sigma.name: Masquerading - Renamed System Binary
Sigma.description: Detects when process Image name differs from OriginalFileName PE field, indicating binary renaming.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

Sysmon.name: Base64 Encoded PowerShell
Sysmon.query: EventID=1 AND Image='*\powershell.exe' AND CommandLine contains '-enc'
Sysmon.description: Detects PowerShell invoked with -enc flag for encoded command execution, a common obfuscation technique.
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Wazuh.name: Encoded PowerShell Execution
Wazuh.description: Wazuh rule detecting encoded PowerShell command execution.
Wazuh.author: Wazuh

Windows Event Log.name: Script Block with Suspicious Content (4104)
Windows Event Log.query: EventID=4104 AND ScriptBlockText contains base64 or encoded strings
Windows Event Log.description: Detects suspicious base64 or encoded content in PowerShell script blocks via EventID 4104.
Windows Event Log.author: Microsoft

Sigma.name: Encoded PowerShell Command
Sigma.description: Sigma rule for detecting encoded PowerShell in process creation logs.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

Suricata.name: Suspicious File Download via HTTP
Suricata.query: alert http any any -> $HOME_NET any (msg:"Suspicious File Download"; fileext:"scr|hta|vbs"; sid:2001571;)
Suricata.description: Detects download of suspicious executable file types (.scr, .hta, .vbs) commonly used in malware delivery.
Suricata.author: Emerging Threats

Sysmon.name: Suspicious File Execution from Download
Sysmon.query: EventID=1 AND Image matches '\Downloads\*.exe' OR '\Temp\*.exe'
Sysmon.description: Detects executables running from Downloads or Temp folders — common for user-executed malware.
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Sigma.name: Execution from Suspicious Directory
Sigma.description: Sigma rule for detecting process execution from user-writable directories.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

Sysmon.name: WMI Process Creation
Sysmon.query: EventID=1 AND ParentImage='*\WmiPrvSE.exe'
Sysmon.description: Detects processes spawned by WmiPrvSE.exe, indicating WMI-based lateral movement or execution.
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Wazuh.name: WMI Remote Execution
Wazuh.description: Wazuh rule detecting WMI-spawned child processes via ParentImage match.
Wazuh.author: Wazuh

Sigma.name: WMI Spawning Process
Sigma.description: Sigma rule for WMI process spawning detection.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

Suricata.name: RDP Connection to Internal Host
Suricata.query: alert tcp any any -> $HOME_NET 3389 (msg:"RDP Connection"; flow:to_server; sid:2001572;)
Suricata.description: Detects RDP connections on port 3389. Low confidence — correlate with logon events for confirmation.
Suricata.author: Emerging Threats

Sysmon.name: RDP Client Execution
Sysmon.query: EventID=3 AND DestinationPort=3389
Sysmon.description: Detects outbound RDP connections via Sysmon network event (EventID 3).
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Windows Event Log.name: RDP Logon (4624 Type 10)
Windows Event Log.description: RDP (RemoteInteractive) logon type — high confidence indicator of interactive remote session.
Windows Event Log.author: Microsoft

Sigma.name: RDP Session Logon
Sigma.description: Sigma rule for detecting RDP logon sessions via EventID 4624 LogonType=10.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

Sysmon.name: Service Stopped via net/sc
Sysmon.query: EventID=1 AND (Image='*\net.exe' OR Image='*\sc.exe') AND CommandLine contains 'stop'
Sysmon.description: Detects service stop commands via net.exe or sc.exe, commonly used by ransomware to disable AV/backup services.
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Wazuh.name: Critical Service Stopped
Wazuh.description: Wazuh rule alerting when Service Control Manager reports a service transition to stopped state.
Wazuh.author: Wazuh

Windows Event Log.name: Service State Change (7036)
Windows Event Log.description: Service Control Manager event for service state change. Monitor for critical service stops.
Windows Event Log.author: Microsoft

Sigma.name: Service Stop via Command Line
Sigma.query: EventID=1 AND CommandLine contains 'net stop' OR 'sc stop'
Sigma.description: Sigma rule detecting service stop commands via command line.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

Sysmon.name: VSS Deletion / bcdedit
Sysmon.query: EventID=1 AND (CommandLine contains 'vssadmin delete shadows' OR CommandLine contains 'bcdedit /set' OR CommandLine contains 'wbadmin delete')
Sysmon.description: Detects shadow copy deletion and backup disabling via vssadmin, bcdedit, or wbadmin — ransomware pre-encryption step.
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Wazuh.name: Shadow Copy Deletion
Wazuh.description: Critical-level Wazuh rule detecting shadow copy deletion commands.
Wazuh.author: Wazuh

Sigma.name: Shadow Copy Deletion
Sigma.query: EventID=1 AND CommandLine contains 'vssadmin delete shadows'
Sigma.description: Sigma rule for vssadmin shadow copy deletion, a near-certain ransomware indicator.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

T1543.003.name: Create or Modify System Process: Windows Service

Sysmon.name: New Service Installation
Sysmon.query: EventID=1 AND Image='*\sc.exe' AND CommandLine contains 'create'
Sysmon.description: Detects new Windows service creation via sc.exe, commonly used for persistence.
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Wazuh.name: Service Created
Wazuh.description: Wazuh rule detecting new service installation via EventID 7045.
Wazuh.author: Wazuh

Windows Event Log.name: New Service Installed (7045)
Windows Event Log.description: A new service was installed. Investigate ServiceFileName for suspicious paths.
Windows Event Log.author: Microsoft

Sigma.name: Suspicious Service Installation
Sigma.description: High-confidence Sigma rule for services installed with cmd/powershell payloads.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

Sysmon.name: Registry Value Set
Sysmon.query: EventID=13
Sysmon.description: Generic registry value write event. Very noisy — must be filtered by TargetObject path.
Sysmon.tuning_hint: Restrict to specific TargetObject paths: Run, Services, Policies, SecurityProviders.
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Wazuh.name: Registry Key Modified
Wazuh.description: Wazuh rule for registry modification events (EventID 4657). Requires registry auditing enabled.
Wazuh.author: Wazuh

Windows Event Log.name: Registry Value Modified (4657)
Windows Event Log.description: Registry object modified. Requires audit policy: Object Access > Audit Registry.
Windows Event Log.author: Microsoft

Sigma.name: Suspicious Registry Modification
Sigma.description: Detects registry writes to persistence-related keys.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ

Sysmon.name: Whoami Execution
Sysmon.query: EventID=1 AND Image='*\whoami.exe'
Sysmon.description: Detects whoami.exe execution used in post-exploitation to identify current user context.
Sysmon.tuning_hint: Correlate with other discovery commands to raise confidence.
Sysmon.author: SigmaHQ

Sigma.name: Whoami Execution
Sigma.description: Low-confidence but useful for correlation; combine with net user, systeminfo events.
Sigma.author: SigmaHQ
